title: Pronunciation: Basic Hindi Sounds
description: Learn the phonetic sounds of Hindi, focusing on the vowels.

blocks.title: Hindi Pronunciation Rules
points: Hindi is perfectly phonetic — you speak exactly what is written.
A/Aa: 'अ' is like 'uh' in 'about'. 'आ' is a long 'Aa' like 'father'.
I/Ee: 'इ' is short 'ih' like 'sit'. 'ई' is long 'ee' like 'see'.
U/Oo: 'उ' is short 'uh' like 'put'. 'ऊ' is long 'oo' like 'boot'.
There are no silent letters in Hindi!

blocks.title: Short vs. Long Vowels
blocks.instruction: Hindi distinguishes between short and long vowels. This can change the meaning of a word!

words.note: Na-MAS-te. The stress often falls on the heaviest syllable (mas).

blocks.title: Hindi Tongue Twister
blocks.tip: Focus on the hard 'k' sounds and the slightly rolled 'r' (ड़ - d/r sound) at the end of paapad.

blocks.label: The first vowels
blocks.transcript: अ (a), आ (aa), इ (i), ई (ee), उ (u), ऊ (oo)
